# AI Description

# Technical Description: Figure A-3.1.1.6.(2)

## 1. Diagram Type
This is a technical detail drawing showing measurement conventions for non-planar glazing systems. It serves as an instructional illustration for code compliance measurement procedures.

## 2. Building Components/Systems Shown
The diagram illustrates three distinct glazing configurations:
- **Bow window** (top)
- **Skylight** (left)
- **Curved window** (right)

## 3. Visible Labels, Measurements, and Annotations

### Labels:
- "bow window" - identifying the multi-segment angled window at top
- "skylight" - identifying the peaked roof glazing element at left
- "curved window" - identifying the arched window element at right
- "EG00733B" - drawing reference number (bottom right)
- "Division B" - code section identifier (top left)
- "A-3.2.1.2-(2)" - specific code reference (top right)

### Title Block Information:
- **Figure A-3.1.1.6.(2)**
- **Title:** "Measuring areas of glazing that is not in the same plane"

## 4. Relationships and Connections Between Elements

### Bow Window:
- Shows a multi-segment window with four vertical glazing panels arranged in an angled configuration
- Double-headed arrows indicate measurement points at:
  - The outer extremities (left and right ends)
  - Between individual glazing segments
- Measurement lines are drawn horizontally across the top, indicating that area should be measured as a projected plane rather than following the actual angled surface

### Skylight:
- Depicts a peaked/gabled skylight configuration with two angled glazing planes meeting at an apex
- Diagonal arrows with measurement indicators point to the corners
- Shows that measurement should be taken at the base opening rather than along the sloped surfaces
- Rectangular base plates shown at mounting points

### Curved Window:
- Illustrates a semi-circular or arched window with curved glazing
- Shows double concentric curves representing the window profile
- Vertical arrows with measurement indicators point downward at both ends
- Indicates measurement should be taken as a straight-line projection rather than following the curve
- Rectangular base plates shown at mounting points

## 5. Compliance Requirements and Technical Specifications

### Measurement Convention:
The diagram establishes the code-compliant method for measuring glazing area when the glazing surface is not in a single plane. The key principle illustrated is:

**For non-planar glazing, measurements should be taken as projected areas in a single reference plane, not as the actual surface area of the angled, curved, or segmented glazing.**

### Application Context:
This measurement convention relates to:
- **Section A-3.1.1.7.(1)(b) - Major Structural Elements:** Defines building envelope penetrating elements (walls, floors, roofs, balconies, joists, beams, girders, columns, curbs)
- **Section A-3.1.1.7.(1)(d) - Secondary Structural Members:** Defines attachment elements (girts, purlins, sub-girts, hat-channels, U-channels, shelf angles) that connect cladding to primary structural members

### Technical Implications:
- Ensures consistent calculation methodology for fenestration area ratios
- Affects building envelope thermal performance calculations
- Impacts compliance with maximum glazing area requirements
- Provides standardized approach for energy modeling inputs
- Prevents overestimation of glazing area that would occur if measuring actual surface area of non-planar installations

This measurement approach is critical for accurate determination of building envelope performance metrics and compliance with prescriptive requirements for fenestration-to-wall area ratios.
